concerns.text: T019 (thresholding logic) is listed before T018 (connectivity matrix computation). T019 consumes the output of T018 (Pearson correlation matrices) to create binary graphs. A consumer task cannot precede its producer. T019 must follow T018.

T020 and T021 (efficiency metrics) are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depend on the output of T019 (binary graphs). While T020 and T021 might be parallel to each other, they cannot run in parallel with T018/T019. The [P] tag on T020/T021 is misleading without explicit dependency on T019 completion, and T022 (multi-resolution) depends on T020/T021 logic. The current linear listing implies T020/T021 run before T019 is complete or ignores the data flow.

T030 (adaptive sampling logic) is placed after T029 (permutation testing). T030 describes the logic to *sample the dataset* to fit time limits. This sampling must occur BEFORE the heavy computation in T029 begins. If T029 runs first, the time limit is already breached. T030 (or the sampling logic it represents) must precede T029.

T031 (sensitivity analysis) is listed after T029 (permutation testing). FR-009 and SC-003 require sensitivity analysis across density thresholds {0.15, 0.20, 0.25}. This implies re-running the graph metric computation (Phase 4) and statistical analysis (Phase 5) for different thresholds. T031 should logically trigger a re-execution of the graph/stats pipeline or be placed before the final report generation (T032) to ensure the sensitivity results are included. Currently, it appears as a post-hoc step that might not feed into the main report generation flow correctly.

T014 (exclusion logic) is listed after T013 (preprocessing). While logically sound (preprocess then check), T014 implies a filtering step that might need to happen before T015 (saving). The current order is acceptable but T014 should explicitly state it filters the dataset *before* T015 saves the final preprocessed time series to ensure only valid subjects are saved.
